meaning: adj.果肉状的,多汁地,柔软地
examples: Her body felt bruised, her lips pulpy and tender.
The soft white pulpy flesh of ripe custard apple is intensely sweet.
THE average American reads pulpy novels in the summer. Bureaucrats read the Federal Register.
By the mid-1970s he had carved out a nice living writing pulpy science fiction and suspense novels.
Coca Cola's Pulpy is now a billion-dollar global brand, but it was first a smash in China.
